Operation 'listPools' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. The current description is too minimal and lacks context about what resource pools are, their purpose, and what the response contains. An AI agent needs to understand that pools are organizational constructs for VMs/containers and what data structure to expect. The suggestion adds domain context, clarifies the return value structure, and explains that all pools are returned regardless of authentication context.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a list of all resource pools configured in the Proxmox VE cluster. Resource pools are logical groupings of virtual machines and containers that can be managed together for resource allocation and access control purposes. Returns an array of pool objects containing pool identifiers, descriptions, and member information. No authentication-specific filtering is applied - all pools are returned.
data.reasoning: The current description is too minimal and lacks context about what resource pools are, their purpose, and what the response contains. An AI agent needs to understand that pools are organizational constructs for VMs/containers and what data structure to expect. The suggestion adds domain context, clarifies the return value structure, and explains that all pools are returned regardless of authentication context.

message: Operation 'getAccessIndex' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Both summary and description are vague and lack specificity. The summary 'Get access control index' is unclear about what an 'index' means in this context. The description doesn't explain what resources are included or what the response structure contains. An AI agent needs to understand this is an API discovery endpoint for access control features. The suggestions clarify the purpose (discovering available access control operations), explain what resources are included, and describe the response structure for better interpretability.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves an index of available access control resources and endpoints in the Proxmox VE system. This operation provides a directory of access control management capabilities including user management, group management, role definitions, and permission configuration endpoints. Returns a structured index object containing available resource paths and their descriptions. This is typically used for API discovery and understanding available access control operations.
data.reasoning: Both summary and description are vague and lack specificity. The summary 'Get access control index' is unclear about what an 'index' means in this context. The description doesn't explain what resources are included or what the response structure contains. An AI agent needs to understand this is an API discovery endpoint for access control features. The suggestions clarify the purpose (discovering available access control operations), explain what resources are included, and describe the response structure for better interpretability.

It lacks context about what the response contains and why an agent would use this operation. data.description_suggestion: Retrieve the API index listing all available top-level resources and endpoints. Returns a structured overview of the Proxmox VE API surface, useful for API discovery and understanding available operations.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate, but description is too brief. It lacks context about what the response contains and why an agent would use this operation. Enhanced description provides better AI agent interpretability by explaining the discovery purpose and response structure.

data.current_description: Return the current Proxmox VE version and other version-related info.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve the current Proxmox VE version and related system information. Returns version number, release details, and other version-related metadata useful for compatibility checks and system diagnostics.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and domain-appropriate. Description is vague about what 'other version-related info' includes. Enhanced description specifies typical return values and use cases, improving AI agent understanding of when and why to call this operation.

message: Operation 'listVMs' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks explicit mention of the required node parameter and what the response contains. Enhanced description clarifies input requirements and output structure for better AI agent decision-making about parameter provision and response handling.

data.current_description: List all QEMU/KVM virtual machines on a node.
data.description_suggestion: List all QEMU/KVM virtual machines on the specified node. Requires the node parameter to identify the target node. Returns an array of VM objects containing VM identifiers, names, status, and configuration details.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks explicit mention of the required node parameter and what the response contains. Enhanced description clarifies input requirements and output structure for better AI agent decision-making about parameter provision and response handling.

message: Operation 'createVM' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks critical details about required inputs (configuration parameters), response format (task-based), and resulting state. Enhanced description specifies input requirements, clarifies asynchronous nature, and explains initial VM state, enabling AI agents to properly construct requests and handle responses.

data.current_description: Create a new QEMU/KVM virtual machine on the specified node.
data.description_suggestion: Create a new QEMU/KVM virtual machine on the specified node. Requires node parameter and VM configuration parameters (vmid, name, memory, cores, etc.). Returns a task response with task ID for tracking the asynchronous creation process. The VM is created in stopped state and must be started separately.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks critical details about required inputs (configuration parameters), response format (task-based), and resulting state. Enhanced description specifies input requirements, clarifies asynchronous nature, and explains initial VM state, enabling AI agents to properly construct requests and handle responses.

message: Operation 'startVM' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description is too minimal about response format and state changes. Enhanced description clarifies the asynchronous nature (task-based response), required parameters, and the resulting state transition, helping AI agents understand how to track the operation and verify completion.

data.description_suggestion: Start the specified virtual machine on the given node. Requires node and vmid parameters to identify the target VM. Returns a task response with task ID for tracking the asynchronous startup process. The VM transitions from stopped to running state.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description is too minimal about response format and state changes. Enhanced description clarifies the asynchronous nature (task-based response), required parameters, and the resulting state transition, helping AI agents understand how to track the operation and verify completion.

message: Operation 'stopVM' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_side_effects, missing_output_details. Current description is too minimal. Added clarity about what identifies the VM, the graceful shutdown behavior, the asynchronous task response, and the resulting state change. This helps AI agents understand the operation's effects and how to track completion.

data.description_suggestion: Stops the specified virtual machine identified by node and vmid. The VM will be gracefully shut down. Returns a task response object that can be used to monitor the stop operation. The VM's state will transition to 'stopped' upon successful completion.
data.reasoning: Current description is too minimal. Added clarity about what identifies the VM, the graceful shutdown behavior, the asynchronous task response, and the resulting state change. This helps AI agents understand the operation's effects and how to track completion.

message: Operation 'getVMStatus' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details. Original description lacks specificity about what status information is returned. Enhanced to clarify the response includes state and performance metrics, helping AI agents understand what data they'll receive and when this operation is useful.

data.current_description: Get the current status of a virtual machine.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the current operational status of a virtual machine on the specified node. Returns a VMStatus object containing the VM's state (running, stopped, paused, etc.), uptime, CPU usage, memory consumption, and other runtime metrics.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks specificity about what status information is returned. Enhanced to clarify the response includes state and performance metrics, helping AI agents understand what data they'll receive and when this operation is useful.

message: Operation 'deleteVM' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_side_effects, missing_context. Critical missing information about irreversibility, prerequisites (VM must be stopped), and comprehensive side effects (deletion of disks, configs, snapshots). AI agents need this context to avoid destructive mistakes and understand the operation's full impact.

data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes the specified virtual machine and all its associated resources including disks, configuration, and snapshots. This operation is irreversible. The VM must be stopped before deletion. Returns a task response object. All data associated with the VM will be removed from storage.
data.reasoning: Critical missing information about irreversibility, prerequisites (VM must be stopped), and comprehensive side effects (deletion of disks, configs, snapshots). AI agents need this context to avoid destructive mistakes and understand the operation's full impact.

message: Operation 'createContainer' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description omits critical details about required/optional parameters, the asynchronous task response, initial container state, and persistence. Enhanced to clarify what inputs are expected, what is returned, and the resulting state, enabling AI agents to properly construct requests and handle responses.

data.current_description: Create a new LXC container on the specified node.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new LXC container on the specified node with the provided configuration parameters (hostname, storage, memory, CPU cores, etc.). Returns a task response object that can be monitored for completion. The container will be created in a stopped state and must be started separately. Configuration is persisted to the node's storage.
data.reasoning: Original description omits critical details about required/optional parameters, the asynchronous task response, initial container state, and persistence. Enhanced to clarify what inputs are expected, what is returned, and the resulting state, enabling AI agents to properly construct requests and handle responses.

message: Operation 'listStorage' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_context. Summary is too generic. Description lacks specificity about response format and storage types. Enhanced both to clarify that this is cluster-wide storage enumeration, what the response contains, and the types of storage backends available, helping AI agents understand the scope and utility of this operation.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a list of all storage resources available in the Proxmox cluster. Returns an array of Storage objects containing information about each storage backend including type (dir, lvm, nfs, etc.), capacity, usage, and availability status. This includes both local and shared storage.
data.reasoning: Summary is too generic. Description lacks specificity about response format and storage types. Enhanced both to clarify that this is cluster-wide storage enumeration, what the response contains, and the types of storage backends available, helping AI agents understand the scope and utility of this operation.

message: Operation 'getClusterIndex' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_output_details, unclear_purpose. Original summary and description are vague and don't clearly convey purpose. 'Index' and 'resources' are ambiguous. Enhanced to clarify this is a cluster-wide overview operation, what categories of information are returned, and typical use cases. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation for cluster discovery versus node-specific operations.

data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve cluster overview and available resources
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves an index of cluster resources and status information. Returns cluster-level metadata including available nodes, storage resources, virtual machines, containers, and cluster configuration. This operation provides a high-level overview of the entire Proxmox cluster and is typically used for cluster discovery and status monitoring.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are vague and don't clearly convey purpose. 'Index' and 'resources' are ambiguous. Enhanced to clarify this is a cluster-wide overview operation, what categories of information are returned, and typical use cases. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation for cluster discovery versus node-specific operations.

Operation 'listPools' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. The current description is too minimal and lacks context about what resource pools are, their purpose, and what the response contains. An AI agent needs to understand that pools are organizational constructs for VMs/containers and what data structure to expect. The suggestion adds domain context, clarifies the return value structure, and explains that all pools are returned regardless of authentication context.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a list of all resource pools configured in the Proxmox VE cluster. Resource pools are logical groupings of virtual machines and containers that can be managed together for resource allocation and access control purposes. Returns an array of pool objects containing pool identifiers, descriptions, and member information. No authentication-specific filtering is applied - all pools are returned.
data.reasoning: The current description is too minimal and lacks context about what resource pools are, their purpose, and what the response contains. An AI agent needs to understand that pools are organizational constructs for VMs/containers and what data structure to expect. The suggestion adds domain context, clarifies the return value structure, and explains that all pools are returned regardless of authentication context.

message: Operation 'getAccessIndex' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Both summary and description are vague and lack specificity. The summary 'Get access control index' is unclear about what an 'index' means in this context. The description doesn't explain what resources are included or what the response structure contains. An AI agent needs to understand this is an API discovery endpoint for access control features. The suggestions clarify the purpose (discovering available access control operations), explain what resources are included, and describe the response structure for better interpretability.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves an index of available access control resources and endpoints in the Proxmox VE system. This operation provides a directory of access control management capabilities including user management, group management, role definitions, and permission configuration endpoints. Returns a structured index object containing available resource paths and their descriptions. This is typically used for API discovery and understanding available access control operations.
data.reasoning: Both summary and description are vague and lack specificity. The summary 'Get access control index' is unclear about what an 'index' means in this context. The description doesn't explain what resources are included or what the response structure contains. An AI agent needs to understand this is an API discovery endpoint for access control features. The suggestions clarify the purpose (discovering available access control operations), explain what resources are included, and describe the response structure for better interpretability.

data.reasoning: Summary is adequate, but description is too brief. It lacks context about what the response contains and why an agent would use this operation. Enhanced description provides better AI agent interpretability by explaining the discovery purpose and response structure.

data.current_description: List all QEMU/KVM virtual machines on a node.
data.description_suggestion: List all QEMU/KVM virtual machines on the specified node. Requires the node parameter to identify the target node. Returns an array of VM objects containing VM identifiers, names, status, and configuration details.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks explicit mention of the required node parameter and what the response contains. Enhanced description clarifies input requirements and output structure for better AI agent decision-making about parameter provision and response handling.

message: Operation 'createVM' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks critical details about required inputs (configuration parameters), response format (task-based), and resulting state. Enhanced description specifies input requirements, clarifies asynchronous nature, and explains initial VM state, enabling AI agents to properly construct requests and handle responses.

data.current_description: Create a new QEMU/KVM virtual machine on the specified node.
data.description_suggestion: Create a new QEMU/KVM virtual machine on the specified node. Requires node parameter and VM configuration parameters (vmid, name, memory, cores, etc.). Returns a task response with task ID for tracking the asynchronous creation process. The VM is created in stopped state and must be started separately.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks critical details about required inputs (configuration parameters), response format (task-based), and resulting state. Enhanced description specifies input requirements, clarifies asynchronous nature, and explains initial VM state, enabling AI agents to properly construct requests and handle responses.

message: Operation 'startVM' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description is too minimal about response format and state changes. Enhanced description clarifies the asynchronous nature (task-based response), required parameters, and the resulting state transition, helping AI agents understand how to track the operation and verify completion.

data.description_suggestion: Start the specified virtual machine on the given node. Requires node and vmid parameters to identify the target VM. Returns a task response with task ID for tracking the asynchronous startup process. The VM transitions from stopped to running state.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description is too minimal about response format and state changes. Enhanced description clarifies the asynchronous nature (task-based response), required parameters, and the resulting state transition, helping AI agents understand how to track the operation and verify completion.

message: Operation 'stopVM' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_side_effects, missing_output_details. Current description is too minimal. Added clarity about what identifies the VM, the graceful shutdown behavior, the asynchronous task response, and the resulting state change. This helps AI agents understand the operation's effects and how to track completion.

data.description_suggestion: Stops the specified virtual machine identified by node and vmid. The VM will be gracefully shut down. Returns a task response object that can be used to monitor the stop operation. The VM's state will transition to 'stopped' upon successful completion.
data.reasoning: Current description is too minimal. Added clarity about what identifies the VM, the graceful shutdown behavior, the asynchronous task response, and the resulting state change. This helps AI agents understand the operation's effects and how to track completion.

message: Operation 'getVMStatus' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details. Original description lacks specificity about what status information is returned. Enhanced to clarify the response includes state and performance metrics, helping AI agents understand what data they'll receive and when this operation is useful.

data.current_description: Get the current status of a virtual machine.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the current operational status of a virtual machine on the specified node. Returns a VMStatus object containing the VM's state (running, stopped, paused, etc.), uptime, CPU usage, memory consumption, and other runtime metrics.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks specificity about what status information is returned. Enhanced to clarify the response includes state and performance metrics, helping AI agents understand what data they'll receive and when this operation is useful.

message: Operation 'deleteVM' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_side_effects, missing_context. Critical missing information about irreversibility, prerequisites (VM must be stopped), and comprehensive side effects (deletion of disks, configs, snapshots). AI agents need this context to avoid destructive mistakes and understand the operation's full impact.

data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes the specified virtual machine and all its associated resources including disks, configuration, and snapshots. This operation is irreversible. The VM must be stopped before deletion. Returns a task response object. All data associated with the VM will be removed from storage.
data.reasoning: Critical missing information about irreversibility, prerequisites (VM must be stopped), and comprehensive side effects (deletion of disks, configs, snapshots). AI agents need this context to avoid destructive mistakes and understand the operation's full impact.

message: Operation 'createContainer' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Enhanced to clarify what inputs are expected, what is returned, and the resulting state, enabling AI agents to properly construct requests and handle responses.

data.current_description: Create a new LXC container on the specified node.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new LXC container on the specified node with the provided configuration parameters (hostname, storage, memory, CPU cores, etc.). Returns a task response object that can be monitored for completion. The container will be created in a stopped state and must be started separately. Configuration is persisted to the node's storage.
data.reasoning: Original description omits critical details about required/optional parameters, the asynchronous task response, initial container state, and persistence. Enhanced to clarify what inputs are expected, what is returned, and the resulting state, enabling AI agents to properly construct requests and handle responses.

message: Operation 'listStorage' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_context. Summary is too generic. Description lacks specificity about response format and storage types. Enhanced both to clarify that this is cluster-wide storage enumeration, what the response contains, and the types of storage backends available, helping AI agents understand the scope and utility of this operation.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a list of all storage resources available in the Proxmox cluster. Returns an array of Storage objects containing information about each storage backend including type (dir, lvm, nfs, etc.), capacity, usage, and availability status. This includes both local and shared storage.
data.reasoning: Summary is too generic. Description lacks specificity about response format and storage types. Enhanced both to clarify that this is cluster-wide storage enumeration, what the response contains, and the types of storage backends available, helping AI agents understand the scope and utility of this operation.

data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve cluster overview and available resources
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves an index of cluster resources and status information. Returns cluster-level metadata including available nodes, storage resources, virtual machines, containers, and cluster configuration. This operation provides a high-level overview of the entire Proxmox cluster and is typically used for cluster discovery and status monitoring.
